speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: Before we proceed any further, hear me

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: speak.

speaker: ALL
text: Speak, speak!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: You are all resolved rather to die than to

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: famish?

speaker: ALL
text: Resolved, resolved!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: First, you know Caius Martius is chief

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: enemy to the people.

speaker: ALL
text: We know ’t, we know ’t!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: Let us kill him, and we’ll have corn at

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: our own price. Is ’t a verdict?

speaker: ALL
text: No more talking on ’t; let it be done. Away, away!

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: One word, good citizens.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: We are accounted poor citizens, the patricians

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: good. What authority surfeits on would

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: relieve us. If they would yield us but the superfluity

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: while it were wholesome, we might guess they

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: relieved us humanely. But they think we are too

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: dear. The leanness that afflicts us, the object of our

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: misery, is as an inventory to particularize their

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: abundance; our sufferance is a gain to them. Let

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: us revenge this with our pikes ere we become

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: rakes; for the gods know I speak this in hunger for

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: bread, not in thirst for revenge.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: Would you proceed especially against

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: Caius Martius?

speaker: ALL
text: Against him first. He’s a very dog to the

speaker: ALL
text: commonalty.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: Consider you what services he has

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: done for his country?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: Very well, and could be content to give

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: him good report for ’t, but that he pays himself

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: with being proud.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: Nay, but speak not maliciously.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: I say unto you, what he hath done

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: famously he did it to that end. Though soft-conscienced

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: men can be content to say it was for

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: his country, he did it to please his mother and to be

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: partly proud, which he is, even to the altitude of

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: his virtue.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: What he cannot help in his nature you

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: account a vice in him. You must in no way say he

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: is covetous.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: If I must not, I need not be barren of accusations.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: He hath faults, with surplus, to tire in

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text:
repetition. 
                (
                Shouts
                 
                within
                .
                )
               What shouts are these?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: The other side o’ th’ city is risen. Why stay we prating

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: here? To th’ Capitol!

speaker: ALL
text: Come, come!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: Soft, who comes here?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: Worthy Menenius Agrippa, one that

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: hath always loved the people.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: He’s one honest enough. Would all the

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: rest were so!

speaker: MENENIUS
text: What work ’s, my countrymen, in hand? Where go you

speaker: MENENIUS
text: With bats and clubs? The matter? Speak, I pray you.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: Our business is not unknown to th’

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: Senate. They have had inkling this fortnight what

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: we intend to do, which now we’ll show ’em in

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: deeds. They say poor suitors have strong breaths;

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: they shall know we have strong arms too.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Why, masters, my good friends, mine honest neighbors,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Will you undo yourselves?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: We cannot, sir; we are undone already.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: I tell you, friends, most charitable care

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Have the patricians of you. For your wants,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Your suffering in this dearth, you may as well

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Strike at the heaven with your staves as lift them

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Against the Roman state, whose course will on

speaker: MENENIUS
text: The way it takes, cracking ten thousand curbs

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Of more strong link asunder than can ever

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Appear in your impediment. For the dearth,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: The gods, not the patricians, make it, and

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Your knees to them, not arms, must help. Alack,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: You are transported by calamity

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Thither where more attends you, and you slander

speaker: MENENIUS
text: The helms o’ th’ state, who care for you like fathers,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: When you curse them as enemies.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: Care for us? True, indeed! They ne’er

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: cared for us yet. Suffer us to famish, and their

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: storehouses crammed with grain; make edicts for

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: usury to support usurers; repeal daily any wholesome

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: act established against the rich, and provide

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: more piercing statutes daily to chain up and restrain

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: the poor. If the wars eat us not up, they will;

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: and there’s all the love they bear us.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Either you must confess yourselves wondrous malicious

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Or be accused of folly. I shall tell you

speaker: MENENIUS
text: A pretty tale. It may be you have heard it,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: But since it serves my purpose, I will venture

speaker: MENENIUS
text: To stale ’t a little more.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: Well, I’ll hear it, sir; yet you must not

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: think to fob off our disgrace with a tale. But, an ’t

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: please you, deliver.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: There was a time when all the body’s members

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Rebelled against the belly, thus accused it:

speaker: MENENIUS
text: That only like a gulf it did remain

speaker: MENENIUS
text: I’ th’ midst o’ th’ body, idle and unactive,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Still cupboarding the viand, never bearing

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Like labor with the rest, where th’ other instruments

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Did see and hear, devise, instruct, walk, feel,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: And, mutually participate, did minister

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Unto the appetite and affection common

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Of the whole body. The belly answered—

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: Well, sir, what answer made the belly?

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Sir, I shall tell you. With a kind of smile,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Which ne’er came from the lungs, but even thus—

speaker: MENENIUS
text: For, look you, I may make the belly smile

speaker: MENENIUS
text: As well as speak—it tauntingly replied

speaker: MENENIUS
text: To th’ discontented members, the mutinous parts

speaker: MENENIUS
text: That envied his receipt; even so most fitly

speaker: MENENIUS
text: As you malign our senators for that

speaker: MENENIUS
text: They are not such as you.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: Your belly’s answer—what?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: The kingly crownèd head, the vigilant eye,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: The counselor heart, the arm our soldier,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: Our steed the leg, the tongue our trumpeter,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: With other muniments and petty helps

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: In this our fabric, if that they—

speaker: MENENIUS
text: What then?

speaker: MENENIUS
text: ’Fore me, this fellow speaks. What then? What then?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: Should by the cormorant belly be restrained,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: Who is the sink o’ th’ body—

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Well, what then?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: The former agents, if they did complain,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: What could the belly answer?

speaker: MENENIUS
text: I will tell you,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: If you’ll bestow a small—of what you have little—

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Patience awhile, you’st hear the belly’s answer.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: You’re long about it.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Note me this, good friend;

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Your most grave belly was deliberate,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Not rash like his accusers, and thus answered:

speaker: MENENIUS
text: “True is it, my incorporate friends,” quoth he,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: “That I receive the general food at first

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Which you do live upon; and fit it is,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Because I am the storehouse and the shop

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Of the whole body. But, if you do remember,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: I send it through the rivers of your blood

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Even to the court, the heart, to th’ seat o’ th’ brain;

speaker: MENENIUS
text: And, through the cranks and offices of man,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: The strongest nerves and small inferior veins

speaker: MENENIUS
text: From me receive that natural competency

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Whereby they live. And though that all at once,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: You, my good friends”—this says the belly, mark me—

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: Ay, sir, well, well.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: “Though all at once cannot

speaker: MENENIUS
text: See what I do deliver out to each,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Yet I can make my audit up, that all

speaker: MENENIUS
text: From me do back receive the flour of all,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: And leave me but the bran.” What say you to ’t?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: It was an answer. How apply you this?

speaker: MENENIUS
text: The senators of Rome are this good belly,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: And you the mutinous members. For examine

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Their counsels and their cares, digest things rightly

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Touching the weal o’ th’ common, you shall find

speaker: MENENIUS
text: No public benefit which you receive

speaker: MENENIUS
text: But it proceeds or comes from them to you

speaker: MENENIUS
text: And no way from yourselves. What do you think,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: You, the great toe of this assembly?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: I the great toe? Why the great toe?

speaker: MENENIUS
text: For that, being one o’ th’ lowest, basest, poorest,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Of this most wise rebellion, thou goest foremost.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Thou rascal, that art worst in blood to run,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Lead’st first to win some vantage.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: But make you ready your stiff bats and clubs.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Rome and her rats are at the point of battle;

speaker: MENENIUS
text: The one side must have bale.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Hail, noble Martius.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Thanks.—What’s the matter, you dissentious rogues,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: That, rubbing the poor itch of your opinion,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Make yourselves scabs?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: We have ever your good word.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: He that will give good words to thee will flatter

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Beneath abhorring. What would you have, you curs,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: That like nor peace nor war? The one affrights you;

speaker: MARTIUS
text: The other makes you proud. He that trusts to you,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Where he should find you lions, finds you hares;

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Where foxes, geese. You are no surer, no,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Than is the coal of fire upon the ice

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Or hailstone in the sun. Your virtue is

speaker: MARTIUS
text: To make him worthy whose offense subdues him,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: And curse that justice did it. Who deserves greatness

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Deserves your hate; and your affections are

speaker: MARTIUS
text: A sick man’s appetite, who desires most that

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Which would increase his evil. He that depends

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Upon your favors swims with fins of lead,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: And hews down oaks with rushes. Hang you! Trust you?

speaker: MARTIUS
text: With every minute you do change a mind

speaker: MARTIUS
text: And call him noble that was now your hate,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Him vile that was your garland. What’s the matter,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: That in these several places of the city

speaker: MARTIUS
text: You cry against the noble senate, who,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Under the gods, keep you in awe, which else

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Would feed on one another?—What’s their seeking?

speaker: MENENIUS
text: For corn at their own rates, whereof they say

speaker: MENENIUS
text: The city is well stored.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Hang ’em! They say?

speaker: MARTIUS
text: They’ll sit by th’ fire and presume to know

speaker: MARTIUS
text: What’s done i’ th’ Capitol, who’s like to rise,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Who thrives, and who declines; side factions and give out

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Conjectural marriages, making parties strong

speaker: MARTIUS
text: And feebling such as stand not in their liking

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Below their cobbled shoes. They say there’s grain enough?

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Would the nobility lay aside their ruth

speaker: MARTIUS
text: And let me use my sword, I’d make a quarry

speaker: MARTIUS
text: With thousands of these quartered slaves as high

speaker: MARTIUS
text: As I could pick my lance.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Nay, these are almost thoroughly persuaded;

speaker: MENENIUS
text: For though abundantly they lack discretion,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Yet are they passing cowardly. But I beseech you,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: What says the other troop?

speaker: MARTIUS
text: They are dissolved. Hang ’em!

speaker: MARTIUS
text: They said they were an-hungry, sighed forth proverbs

speaker: MARTIUS
text: That hunger broke stone walls, that dogs must eat,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: That meat was made for mouths, that the gods sent not

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Corn for the rich men only. With these shreds

speaker: MARTIUS
text: They vented their complainings, which being answered

speaker: MARTIUS
text: And a petition granted them—a strange one,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: To break the heart of generosity

speaker: MARTIUS
text: And make bold power look pale—they threw their caps

speaker: MARTIUS
text: As they would hang them on the horns o’ th’ moon,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Shouting their emulation.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: What is granted them?

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Five tribunes to defend their vulgar wisdoms,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Of their own choice. One’s Junius Brutus,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Sicinius Velutus, and I know not. ’Sdeath!

speaker: MARTIUS
text: The rabble should have first unroofed the city

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Ere so prevailed with me. It will in time

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Win upon power and throw forth greater themes

speaker: MARTIUS
text: For insurrection’s arguing.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: This is strange.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Go get you home, you fragments.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Where’s Caius Martius?

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Here. What’s the matter?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The news is, sir, the Volsces are in arms.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: I am glad on ’t. Then we shall ha’ means to vent

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Our musty superfluity.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: See our best elders.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Martius, ’tis true that you have lately told us:

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: The Volsces are in arms.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: They have a leader,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Tullus Aufidius, that will put you to ’t.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: I sin in envying his nobility,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: And, were I anything but what I am,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: I would wish me only he.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: You have fought together?

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Were half to half the world by th’ ears and he

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Upon my party, I’d revolt, to make

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Only my wars with him. He is a lion

speaker: MARTIUS
text: That I am proud to hunt.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Then, worthy Martius,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Attend upon Cominius to these wars.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: It is your former promise.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Sir, it is,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: And I am constant.—Titus Lartius, thou

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Shalt see me once more strike at Tullus’ face.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: What, art thou stiff? Stand’st out?

speaker: LARTIUS
text: No, Caius Martius,

speaker: LARTIUS
text: I’ll lean upon one crutch and fight with t’ other

speaker: LARTIUS
text: Ere stay behind this business.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: O, true bred!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Your company to th’ Capitol, where I know

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Our greatest friends attend us.

speaker: LARTIUS
text: Lead you on.—

speaker: LARTIUS
text:
To
                 
                Martius
                .
                 
              Follow Cominius. We must follow you;

speaker: LARTIUS
text: Right worthy you priority.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Noble Martius.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Hence to your homes, begone.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Nay, let them follow.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: The Volsces have much corn; take these rats thither

speaker: MARTIUS
text: To gnaw their garners.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Worshipful mutineers,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Your valor puts well forth.—Pray follow.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Was ever man so proud as is this Martius?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: He has no equal.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: When we were chosen tribunes for the people—

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Marked you his lip and eyes?

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Nay, but his taunts.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Being moved, he will not spare to gird the gods—

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Bemock the modest moon.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: The present wars devour him! He is grown

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Too proud to be so valiant.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Such a nature,

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Tickled with good success, disdains the shadow

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Which he treads on at noon. But I do wonder

speaker: SICINIUS
text: His insolence can brook to be commanded

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Under Cominius.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Fame, at the which he aims,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: In whom already he’s well graced, cannot

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Better be held nor more attained than by

speaker: BRUTUS
text: A place below the first; for what miscarries

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Shall be the General’s fault, though he perform

speaker: BRUTUS
text: To th’ utmost of a man, and giddy censure

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Will then cry out of Martius “O, if he

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Had borne the business!”

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Besides, if things go well,

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Opinion that so sticks on Martius shall

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Of his demerits rob Cominius.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Come.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Half all Cominius’ honors are to Martius,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Though Martius earned them not, and all his faults

speaker: BRUTUS
text: To Martius shall be honors, though indeed

speaker: BRUTUS
text: In aught he merit not.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Let’s hence and hear

speaker: SICINIUS
text: How the dispatch is made, and in what fashion,

speaker: SICINIUS
text: More than his singularity, he goes

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Upon this present action.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Let’s along.
